ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Project Haneul Term Sheet — Strategic Investment and Acquisition in the Republic of Korea

Transaction: KRW 48,000,000,000 private placement of convertible bonds and a call option to acquire 70% of Pinebit Keeper Co., a digital-asset custody business (DPT-related), with closing targeted for 90 days post-signing.

Use of proceeds: fund the acquisition and implement climate-risk mitigation upgrades to Seonam Tech’s data centers (Scope 2 renewable PPA pilot and grid-interactive cooling), items currently not publicly disclosed.

Regulatory: closing subject to applicable provisions of the Capital Markets Act of Korea, notification to the Digital Asset Reporting Bureau, and any directive of the Korean Financial Compliance Authority; any material change in relevant regulations allows good-faith renegotiation.

Disclosure and conduct: no selective disclosure or tipping; all public statements coordinated through the issuer’s designated disclosure officer and released via the national disclosure system at signing or closing as appropriate.

Exclusivity and break fee: 60-day exclusivity from LOI; no-shop and standstill apply to competing crypto-custody or ESG-linked financing proposals, with a KRW 1,200,000,000 break fee upon willful breach.

Project Haneul Term Sheet — Strategic Investment and Acquisition in the Republic of Korea
Parties: Issuer — Seonam Tech Co., Ltd.; Investor — Mirinae Capital Partners SPC; Target — Pinebit Keeper Co. (all entities are independent and incorporated in Korea).
Transaction: KRW 48,000,000,000 private placement of convertible bonds and a call option to acquire 70% of Pinebit Keeper Co., a digital-asset custody business (DPT-related), with closing targeted for 90 days post-signing.
Use of proceeds: fund the acquisition and implement climate-risk mitigation upgrades to Seonam Tech’s data centers (Scope 2 renewable PPA pilot and grid-interactive cooling), items currently not publicly disclosed.
Diligence: review of Seonam Tech’s internal climate scenario analysis prepared for the upcoming Q3 IR draft and Pinebit Keeper’s key management policies for custody operations, none of which has been released to the market.
Pricing and lock-up: CB coupon 3.25% p.a., conversion price KRW 9,800 per share, valuation cap KRW 220,000,000,000; 12-month lock-up and a 90-day post-conversion blackout window for insiders.
Regulatory: closing subject to applicable provisions of the Capital Markets Act of Korea, notification to the Digital Asset Reporting Bureau, and any directive of the Korean Financial Compliance Authority; any material change in relevant regulations allows good-faith renegotiation.
Information controls: each party will maintain an information barrier; only wall-crossed personnel may receive the non-public IR deck “Project Baram,” and a contemporaneous insider list shall be kept and provided to the competent authority upon request.
Disclosure and conduct: no selective disclosure or tipping; all public statements coordinated through the issuer’s designated disclosure officer and released via the national disclosure system at signing or closing as appropriate.
Cyber incident CP: satisfactory remediation of Seonam Tech’s 19 May 2026 ransomware incident, with external auditor comfort that no customer personal data (including any resident-number data) was accessed; redacted forensic report to be provided to Investor under NDA.
Data privacy: the parties shall not exchange or process resident-registration numbers; any HR diligence will use anonymized employee IDs in accordance with PIPA and internal protocols.
Exclusivity and break fee: 60-day exclusivity from LOI; no-shop and standstill apply to competing crypto-custody or ESG-linked financing proposals, with a KRW 1,200,000,000 break fee upon willful breach.
Crypto-specific conditions: Pinebit Keeper to maintain VASP-equivalent registration status and implement cold-storage segregation and MPC policies; any adverse supervisory finding on safekeeping controls is a termination right for Investor.
Commercial protections: MAC clause covering material cyber incidents, regulatory bans on DPT custody, or loss of key clients representing over 20% of Pinebit Keeper’s fee income; 10% of purchase price to be held in escrow for 12 months for indemnity.
Educational/marketing note: for educational outreach only, the issuer’s marketing brochure describes tokenization case studies and information-barrier training scenarios that are generic and do not contain MNPI or offer any DPTs.
Contacts: Primary — Mr. Jae-Min Ryu, Director of Corporate Strategy, ir@seonamtech.test; Investor liaison — Ms. Hyo-Jin Kwon, Partner at Mirinae Capital, deals@mirinaecap.test
